prose:
Frontier-orbital gap (Koopmans HOMO–LUMO gap) of furan

Property        : HOMO–LUMO gap = 6.2573602 eV (energy difference between LUMO and HOMO)
Level of theory : DFT, tier ‘standard’ → B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fitted (PySCF backend)
Geometry source : User-supplied /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/furan/furan.xyz; single-point only (no geometry re-optimization) ⇒ notation B3LYP/def2-TZVP//furan.xyz
Solvent model   : Gas phase (no implicit solvation)
Charge/Multiplicity : 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet, RKS)
Software        : PySCF (via chemkit ‘frontier-orbitals’ skill)
SCF convergence : Converged in 9 cycles; scf_converged = true
Warnings        : no warnings reported
Live log file   : chemkit writes a timestamped frontier_<timestamp>.out log to the working directory and streams it live during execution; consult that file for full SCF and integral-engine details.

Selected orbital energies (eV):
  HOMO-2  –10.332024
  HOMO-1   –7.8707441
  HOMO     –6.3597638
  LUMO     –0.1024036
  LUMO+1   +1.4483308
  LUMO+2   +1.7023780

Headline result: HOMO–LUMO gap = 6.2573602 eV.

This result was computed (not experimental) with chemkit’s frontier-orbitals task at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level in the gas phase; integrity check status: ok, trustworthy = true.
